Patch Set 2: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

(1 inline comment)

please answer my question. actually I put that long ago, but didn't realize I have to publish it ...

Patch Set 2: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

It seems kind of sad to only have a 1-entry cache for the OSD.  This may help some limited benchmark, but it doesn't seem practical for real-world usage when there are hundreds of clients accessing the OSD at once.

What about using a small lustre hash for this?

Patch Set 2:

Andreas, we do need one-entry cache only for this process to populate lu-cache in few cycles. all other processes will find the object in lu-cache by fid.

Patch Set 2: Looks good to me, but someone else must approve

I retract my previous comment.  I didn't notice that it was getting the cache item from "oti" for each thread.

Patch Set 2: Abandoned

Consider OI Scrub, when osd_ea_lookup(), we need to return both FID and ino/gen to the caller for further consistency checking and updating, depends on the configuration, we can skip the subsequent OI lookup when necessary.

So the OSD layer per-thread one entry cache for 'FID=>ino/gen" mapping is unnecessary now. Related function(s) will be implemented in ARI-2

Patch Set 2:

to which caller we need to return ino/generation?

Patch Set 2:

>Patch Set 2:
>to which caller we need to return ino/generation?

To any lookup-by-name caller that can use these informations to check whether the "FID=>inode" mapping in the OI is correct or not, which will be part the of OI Scrub project.

Patch Set 2:

I don't think we can do this - we're going to use this API on different backends (ldiskfs, zfs, etc). not all of them have the same notion of inode.

Patch Set 2:

> Patch Set 2:
> I don't think we can do this - we're going to use this API on different backends (ldiskfs, zfs, etc). not all of them have the same notion of inode.

We have to, because it is part of Opensfs contract to resolve the MDT file-level backup/restore issues.

We can transfer these informations among MDS stack as opaque parameter (if needed), and just let the special OSD to process it internally. Anyway, the one-entry cache for "FID<=>inode" mapping in OSD can be processed by some OI Scrub logic.

Patch Set 2:

why would we need to transfer this information among MDS stack? AFAIU, all we need is to scan local objects and rebuild OI. all this can be done internally within OSD.

Patch Set 2:

> Patch Set 2:
> why would we need to transfer this information among MDS stack? AFAIU, all we need is to scan local objects and rebuild OI. all this can be done internally within OSD.

We plan to support online OI Scrub, which allows client to perform lookup-by-name as normal during the OI file rebuilding, which requires the lookup-by-name sponsor can verify whether the OI file is valid or not by itself. Currently, the 'lookup-by-name' on MDS is split into as two steps: the first step is 'name-to-FID', the second one is 'FID-to-inode'. What we want to verify is in the second step, but we need the information (ino/gen) from the first step. That is why we want to transfer these information among MDS stack. But on the other hand, I am also thinking whether we can verify the OI consistency at the first step, if yes, then need transfer nothing among MDS stack, that is why I say "(if needed)" in last comment, not make decision yet.

Patch Set 2:

if I remember correctly we have a patch implementing this already.

Patch Set 2:

> Patch Set 2:
> if I remember correctly we have a patch implementing this already.

Really? Where? If yes, it save us a lot of time for the same money ;)

Patch Set 2:

seems I posted the message to the wrong ticket ... so repeat: 
https://bugzilla.lustre.org/show_bug.cgi?id=22741 - probably not finished, but better than nothing.

parents.subject: No bugzilla for this. One of the test failing was config-sanity.sh test number 37. So I tracked it down to way. The reason was the mounts were failing. Upon investigation I discovered the test caught a error reported back by the mount. I tracked it down to the section of code in the patch. Basically mount_lustre.c attempts to set the stripe_cache_size for /dev/mdX devices. The problem is this extra feature is present for certain classes of md devices. In the case of our test bed we are one of the classes that lacks this enhancement. So instead of failing to mount in that case we just print a warning that the special param is not present but still succed to mount.

commit.subject: ORNL-5 Cache mapping fid to ino in osd
commit.message:
ORNL-5 Cache mapping fid to ino in osd

Cache mapping for fid<=>ino in osd to drop unnecessary OI (IAM)
lookup operation on MDS for processing cold lookup RPC

Signed-off-by: nasf <yong.fan@whamcloud.com>
Change-Id: Ifb4be7be56e634b28bc13d0871b307a0b3de3577
